Symptoms: sneezing, runny nose, sore throat, cough, mild fever
Description: The common cold is a viral infection of your nose and throat.
Prevention: Wash hands frequently, avoid close contact with sick people, and stay warm.

Symptoms: high fever, chills, body aches, fatigue, dry cough
Description: The flu is a contagious respiratory illness caused by influenza viruses.
Prevention: Get a flu shot annually, rest well, and stay hydrated.

Symptoms: fever, dry cough, tiredness, loss of taste, difficulty breathing
Description: COVID-19 is a contagious disease caused by the coronavirus SARS-CoV-2.
Prevention: Wear a mask, maintain social distance, and get vaccinated.

Symptoms: headache, nausea, sensitivity to light, blurred vision
Description: Migraine is a neurological condition that causes severe headaches.
Prevention: Avoid triggers, maintain sleep hygiene, and manage stress.

Symptoms: frequent urination, increased thirst, blurred vision, fatigue
Description: A chronic condition that affects how your body processes blood sugar.
Prevention: Maintain a healthy diet, exercise regularly, and monitor blood sugar.

Symptoms: shortness of breath, wheezing, coughing, chest tightness
Description: Asthma is a condition in which your airways narrow and swell, producing extra mucus.
Prevention: Avoid allergens, use prescribed inhalers, and monitor breathing regularly.

Symptoms: high blood pressure, headache, dizziness, nosebleeds
Description: Hypertension is a condition where the blood pressure in the arteries is persistently elevated.
Prevention: Reduce salt intake, exercise regularly, and manage stress.

Description: Malaria is a disease caused by a plasmodium parasite, transmitted by the bite of infected mosquitoes.
Prevention: Use mosquito nets, take antimalarial medications, and avoid mosquito-prone areas.

Symptoms: persistent cough, weight loss, fever, night sweats
Description: Tuberculosis is a serious infectious disease that mainly affects the lungs.
Prevention: Get vaccinated (BCG), avoid exposure to infected individuals, and maintain good hygiene.

Symptoms: high fever, severe headache, joint pain, rash, bleeding gums
Description: Dengue is a mosquito-borne viral disease occurring in tropical and subtropical areas.
Prevention: Prevent mosquito bites, eliminate standing water, and use mosquito repellents.

Symptoms: heartburn, chest pain, regurgitation, sour taste in mouth
Description: Acid reflux occurs when stomach acid flows back into the esophagus, causing irritation.
Prevention: Avoid spicy food, eat smaller meals, don’t lie down immediately after eating.

Symptoms: infrequent stools, hard stool, abdominal pain, bloating
Description: Constipation refers to difficulty in emptying the bowels, often associated with hardened feces.
Prevention: Increase fiber intake, stay hydrated, and exercise regularly.

Symptoms: dry mouth, dizziness, dark urine, fatigue
Description: Dehydration happens when you lose more fluids than you take in.
Prevention: Drink plenty of fluids, especially in hot weather or during physical activity.

Description: Food poisoning is caused by consuming contaminated food or drinks.
Prevention: Practice safe food handling, cook food thoroughly, and store food properly.

Symptoms: toothache, sensitivity, visible holes in teeth, pain while eating
Description: Tooth decay is the destruction of your tooth enamel caused by bacteria and poor oral hygiene.
Prevention: Brush twice daily, floss regularly, avoid sugary food, and visit the dentist regularly.
